privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.intro: This Privacy Notice explains how Kaji handles information in connection with the Kaji website and macOS application.

sections.heading: Our role in processing data
paragraphs: Kaji is designed as a local operations layer for terminal-native coding agents. Your repositories, code, terminal output, and agent history stay on systems you control unless you choose to send data to a third-party provider or integration.

When the website collects basic analytics or contact information, Kaji acts as a controller. When the app helps you operate local tools and agent CLIs, those tools and providers remain governed by their own terms.

sections.heading: Information we collect
paragraphs: We may collect contact details you choose to provide, website usage information, download and update metadata, support messages, and limited product diagnostics such as crash reports or feature usage signals.
Kaji does not need to upload your codebase or terminal history to provide its core local workspace features.

sections.heading: How we use information
bullets: Provide, maintain, secure, and improve Kaji
Respond to support requests and product feedback
Understand reliability, adoption, and feature usage
Communicate product updates and security notices
Comply with legal obligations and protect the product

sections.heading: AI providers and integrations
paragraphs: If you use Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, Pi, or another integration through Kaji, the data you send to that provider is handled under your relationship with that provider. Kaji does not control how third-party model providers process data you send to them.

sections.heading: Security and retention
paragraphs: We use reasonable technical and organizational safeguards for information we process. We retain personal data only as long as needed for the purposes described here unless a longer period is required by law.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For privacy questions, contact the Kaji team through the project repository or the support channel listed on the website.

terms.title: Customer Terms and Conditions
terms.intro: These Customer Terms and Conditions govern access to and use of Kaji, the native macOS command center for coding agents.

sections.heading: Welcome to Kaji
paragraphs: By downloading, installing, or using Kaji, you agree to these terms. If you use Kaji on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have authority to bind that organization.
Kaji is provided to help you operate local repositories, worktrees, terminal panes, agent sessions, diffs, and verification workflows.

sections.heading: Use of the product
bullets: You are responsible for your repositories, credentials, prompts, commands, and outputs
You must use Kaji in compliance with applicable law and third-party provider terms
You must not use Kaji to compromise systems, bypass access controls, or infringe third-party rights
You are responsible for reviewing generated code and verification results before shipping changes

sections.heading: Local software and third-party services
paragraphs: Kaji coordinates local tools and third-party agent CLIs. Those tools may have separate licenses, accounts, rate limits, and data practices. Kaji does not replace your agreements with those services.
Some features may require installed command-line tools, git repositories, macOS permissions, or provider authentication configured by you.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
paragraphs: Kaji and its related product materials are owned by their respective rights holders. You retain ownership of your code and content, subject to any third-party terms that apply to services you choose to use.

sections.heading: Disclaimers
paragraphs: Kaji is provided as-is unless a separate written agreement says otherwise. Coding agents can make mistakes. You are responsible for reviewing commands, code, diffs, and verification before relying on them.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: For legal or product questions, contact the Kaji team through the project repository or the support channel listed on the website.
